rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MARILYN ANN MALLEN TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Demurrer To Petition For Order Removing Trustees (Added For Posting Of The Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: This tentative is issued by Judge Ross C. Moody. Respondents' unopposed request for judicial notice is GRANTED. Notice is limited to the relevant facts properly subject to notice given the materials presented and does not, for example, extend to the truth of assertions in pleadings or transcripts. (See Richtek USA, Inc. v. UPI Semiconductor Corp. (2015) 242 Cal.App.4th 651, 659-660.) Respondents' Demurrer to Daniel Mallen's Petition for Order Removing Trustee's, Etc., brought specifically as being barred by the statutes of limitation of Code of Civil Procedure sections 337, 338(d), 343, 366.2, and 366.3 and generally as a demurrer for failure to state cause of action, is OVERRULED. The instant Petition does not seek relief for the causes of action for which Respondents have alleged that the statutes of limitation have expired. Rather, the Petition alleges that the actions taken by Respondents as agents under the Durable Power of Attorney are evidence of their unfitness as trustees, or constitute good cause for removing of Respondents as trustees. The standards for evaluating a demurrer are well understood. In considering a demurrer, one admits the truth of all ultimate facts alleged in the pleading; all properly alleged facts must be admitted as true for purposes of ruling on a demurrer, regardless of how improbable or unlikely such facts might seem. See Blank v. Kirwan, 39 Cal. 3d 311, 318 (1985); Serrano v. Priest, 5 Cal. 3d 584, 591 (1971); see also Del E. Webb Corp. v. Structural Materials Co., 123 Cal. App. 3d 593, 604 (1981). Moreover, the pleader's ability to prove the allegations in the petition is not at issue in a demurrer. Gervase v. Superior Court, 31 Cal. App. 4th 1218, 1224 (1995). Assuming true the facts properly pleaded and facts properly noticed, do the challenged claims necessarily fail? (See Code Civ. Proc., section 430.10(e); Blank v. Kirwan, supra, 39 Cal.3d at p. 318.) "[I]f, on consideration of all facts stated, it appears that plaintiff is entitled to any relief against defendant, complaint will be held good, though facts may not be clearly stated, or may be intermingled with statement of other facts irrelevant to cause of action shown, or though plaintiff may demand relief to which he is not entitled under facts alleged." (Augustine v. Trucco (1954) 124 Cal.App.2d 229, 236, quoting Matteson v. Wagoner (1905) 147 Cal. 739, 742.) Here, the prayer seeks the removal and replacement of the trustees, the bringing of the trust under court supervision, and the requirement of bond. On the face of the pleading, Petitioner alleges that Respondents are unfit to serve as trustees and there is other good cause for their removal. Probate Code Section 15642 subdivision (b) provides that the "grounds for removal of a trustee by the court include" where the trustee is "insolvent or otherwise unfit to administer the trust" or for "other good cause." (Section 15642(b)(2) and (9).) The removal grounds of "unfit" and "for other good cause" under section 15642 are extremely broad. The question whether a trustee's actions require removal is one of fact for the trial court and dependent upon the circumstances of each particular case. Generally, the trustee may be removed by the court for misconduct, unfitness, or acquisition of an adverse interest. "The court has the inherent power to remove a trustee where necessary to preserve the trust and protect beneficiaries. Removal is one of the remedies for breach of trust, but may also be appropriate where the trustee lacks capacity to administer the trust, has committed a crime involving dishonesty, is unfit due to senility, drunkenness, or lack of ability, is absent, unreasonably fails to cooperate with cotrustees, or shows favoritism toward some beneficiaries." (18 Cal. Law Rev. Com. Reports, p. 533.)

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MARILYN ANN MALLEN TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Demurrer To Daniel Mallens Petition For Order Removing Trustees, Etc.
rulings.Rulings: Here, assuming the truth of all material facts properly pleaded in the Petition, the court cannot conclude that Petitioner will be unable to obtain the relief sought on a viable legal theory. This is a sufficient showing to overcome Respondents' demurrer. The court makes no judgment regarding whether the staleness of the allegations is significant as that is not appropriate for review on demurrer. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on September 22, 2021. Petitioner is ordered to lodge with the clerk in Department 204 a proposed order consistent with this tentative by the time set for this hearing. Remote appearance is strongly encouraged, in conformity with the current General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division, available on the court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/divisions/probate. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204.
